title: Small Teams vs. Big Teams: Productivity & Meeting Madness! #shorts
summary: Small agile teams are more productive and efficient than large teams, which get bogged down in meetings and coordination overhead. The video argues that efficiency and task completion favor smaller organizational units.
writeup: This short from Cyril Imhof contrasts small agile teams with large teams on productivity. The argument: large teams lose time to meetings and coordination overhead, while smaller units complete tasks more efficiently. The takeaway is that efficiency and task completion favor smaller organizational units.
